meaning: [单复同]n.林吉特(马来西亚货币单位)
examples: The Malaysian ringgit can now trade directly with the yuan.
The Korean won has slipped about 1% this year while the Malaysian ringgit has dropped around 2.4%.
It is also known as ringgit locally in Malay. The exchange rate is around 1 USD for 1.65 Brunei dollars.
He defied the global institution and, instead, linked the Malaysian ringgit to the dollar.
Malaysian infrastructure builder YTL Corp. recently raised capital locally through a bond placement denominated in ringgit.
